items.title: Newcastle agree £34m deal for Monaco's Bamba
items.summary: Karim Adeyemi moves from Dortmund in £19m deal Newcastle close on £34m Aladji Bamba from Monaco Barcelona have signed German winger Karim Adeyemi from Borussia Dortmund ⁠on a deal until 2031 while Newcastle are set to make Aladji Bamba their fourth summer signing. The La Liga club will pay around €22m (£19m) plus add-ons for the Germany international ⁠and the package includes a sell-on clause.

items.title: Arsenal sign Greece forward Tzolis for £34m
items.summary: Tzolis joins Hincapié and Meslier among arrivals ‘⁠It’s absolutely amazing to sign for such a big ​club’ Arsenal have completed the £34m signing of Christos Tzolis from Club Brugge. The Greece international, 24, has signed a five-year contract with the Premier League champions and arrives as a replacement for Leandro Trossard following his switch to Besiktas. Tzolis’ move to the Emirates Stadium – Arsenal’s third permanent transfer of the summer – arrives 48 hours after the north Londoners missed out on Morgan Rogers who signed a £117m British-record fee with Chelsea, describing his new employers as the “biggest club in London” .

items.title: Norway consider complaint over Balogun World Cup ban suspension
items.summary: Norwegian federation president: Fifa on a ‘slippery slope’ Trump called Infantino for review of Balogun’s red Donald Trump’s role in clearing Folarin Balogun from a mandatory one-game ban at the World Cup is set to provoke a formal complaint to Fifa ethics investigators. The Norway soccer federation said Thursday its board of directors will consider filing the complaint at its next meeting, likely on 6 August.

items.title: Hull City owner 'disturbed' by transfer claims
items.summary: Hull City owner Acun Ilicali says he is "disturbed" by claims of the club being rejected by transfer targets and, in an unusual move, attempted to clarify the situation

items.title: Leeds in talks to sign Man City keeper Trafford
items.summary: Leeds United are in talks with Manchester City over a deal for goalkeeper James Trafford

items.title: Inter Miami under investigation after signing Casemiro
items.summary: Midfielder signs a one-year deal with extension option League investigation centers on the transfer Inter Miami have signed Casemiro as a free agent, but Major League Soccer is investigating the South Florida side for potential tampering in the process of acquiring the Brazilian midfielder. Casemiro has signed what amounts to a one-year contract with Miami, beginning now and ending at the conclusion of the 2027 “sprint season” that precedes the league’s switch to a fall-to-spring calendar in 2027-28. There is an option for an extension that would see the Brazilian stay in Miami through the 2028-29 MLS season.

items.title: Saliba to miss extended period with back injury
items.summary: Centre-back forced off in World Cup semi-final Arsenal say problem does not require surgery Arsenal, the Premier League champions, have been dealt a big injury blow after the centre-back William Saliba was ruled out for “an extended period” with a back injury. Saliba played with a back problem during France’s run to the World Cup semi-final and, although he will not require an operation, the 25-year-old is set for a significant spell on the sidelines. He was forced off with the injury after just 30 minutes of France’s last-four defeat by Spain .

items.title: Villa agree loan deal for Chelsea winger Garnacho
items.summary: Aston Villa agree a loan deal for Chelsea winger Alejandro Garnacho until the end of the 2026-27 season, with an obligation to buy

items.title: Foden signs new four-year Man City contract
items.summary: 26-year-old looking forward to working with Maresca ‘To stay this long at the club is a dream’ Phil Foden has signed a new four-year contract with Manchester City, saying he is looking forward to “trying to win more” at his boyhood club. The deal, which has an additional 12-month option, ties the 26-year-old to the club until 2030. Foden is relishing working again with Enzo Maresca.

items.title: Southampton boss Eckert charged by FA over Spygate
items.summary: 33-year-old German could be hit with one-year ban South-coast club denied chance to win promotion Southampton’s manager, Tonda Eckert, is facing a lengthy suspension from all football activity after being charged with three counts of misconduct by the Football Association. They relate to the Spygate scandal and involve breaching FA rules during covert missions to illicitly observe Championship training sessions involving Middlesbrough, Ipswich and Oxford last season.

items.title: Klart: Amin Boudri till Hammarby
items.summary: Sent på onsdagskvällen blev det som många väntat sig klart. 21-åringen köps loss från Los Angeles FC och uppges bli Allsvenskans dyraste värvning någonsin

items.title: Rebecca Peterson opererad: ”Ska tillbaka starkare”
items.summary: Den förra Sverige-ettan Rebecca Peterson har genomgått en operation och siktar på att komma tillbaka. ”Jag ser framemot att komma tillbaka starkare”, skriver Peterson

items.title: Loreta Kullashi återvänder till AIK efter nio år
items.summary: På torsdagsmorgonen presenterade AIK landslagsmeriterade anfallaren Loreta Kullashi på fri transfer. – Jag är jätteglad att vara här, säger hon till klubbens hemsida

items.title: Arsenal complete deal to sign winger Tzolis
items.summary: In Christos Tzolis, Arsenal's first outfield signing since becoming Premier League champions is a fairly surprising one. But nobody will be more stunned about this deal than Norwich fans

items.title: Arsenal interested in Stones, Konsa after Saliba injury blow
items.summary: Arsenal hold an interest in John Stones and Ezri Konsa as they look to sign a centre-back this summer following news of an extended period on the sidelines for William Saliba

items.title: Why do Chelsea want to sign Lacroix from Palace?
items.summary: Chelsea have not paid a transfer fee for a player aged 26 or over since the summer of 2022 with the ill-fated acquisitions of Raheem Sterling and Kalidou Koulibaly. But Crystal Palace’s Maxence Lacroix is a player worth relaxing the policy for

items.title: Stängs av tre matcher efter strypgreppet
items.summary: Under helgens superettanmatch mellan Östers IF och IK Brage tog Brages Anton Lundin ett strypgrepp på Linus Carlstrand. Nu har disciplinnämnden beslutat att Lundin ska stängas av i tre matcher

items.title: Manchester United consider Camavinga and Berge as midfield overhaul continues
items.summary: Real Madrid and Fulham players on club’s radar United have already signed two midfielders this summer Manchester United have identified Real Madrid’s Eduardo Camavinga and Fulham’s Sander Berge as midfield signings to further strengthen Michael Carrick’s squad. United have already signed two midfielders this window: Andrey Santos in a £48m deal from Chelsea and Youri Tielemans for £35m from Aston Villa . But with Casemiro having departed and Manuel Ugarte ruled out for what may be a prolonged period due to knee ligament injury, the club is considering moving for another midfielder.

items.title: Liverpool primed for business: Bezos, Bhatia and the next steps at Anfield
items.summary: Fenway Sports Group looks to be locked in talks with a consortium led by the son-in-law of Lakshmi Mittal, while the Amazon founder has now been linked Continue reading

items.title: Trossard, Ramos, Dumfries: 10 transfers you may have missed during the World Cup
items.summary: Too engrossed with the World Cup drama to keep up with goings-on in the transfer market? We’ve got you covered Announced just before England’s soul-sucking semi-final defeat by Argentina , it would have been easy to miss Trossard’s £15.3m move to Istanbul. While, after three and a half seasons and at the age of 31, it feels like his time at Arsenal has come to a natural conclusion, he will be remembered as a player who scored in clutch moments and, ultimately, played a crucial role in the club’s first league title in 22 years .

items.title: England cannot afford trip to amputee World Cup – and the FA will not help
items.summary: With no national funding, England’s successful men’s amputee side is struggling to raise the £50,000 needed for November’s tournament in Mexico Every time Scott Rogers walks into St George’s Park there is a sign on the wall that catches his eye. It boasts that the 330-acre site is the proud home of all England’s football teams. “I look at it every time and go, ‘Well, not all of them’” Rogers says.

items.title: Football Daily | Here are 48 reasons to cherish the World Cup (minus the Geopolitics)
items.summary: The Geopolitics World Cup is already fading from view, replaced by a thin July gruel of pre-season friendlies and transfer tales. But there’s still time to cast another look back over the 48-team, 104-game, 308-goal leviathan that has dominated the last six weeks of our lives. Without further ado, here are 48 reasons to cherish this year’s World Cup (minus the Geopolitics).

items.title: ‘Where did they go?’: homeless people feel force of America’s brutality in World Cup clean-up
items.summary: Fifa says football brings the world together but the unhoused in Atlanta feel targeted and completely excluded from the tournament “A lot of our community has been pushed out by the World Cup. We’re not just dollar signs, we’re more than that. We’re people and we’re frustrated that they’ve chosen to treat us less than human.” “ They dropped me off there in the middle of the night.

items.title: Panelen om Boudri i Bajen: ”Klubbkänsla=noll”
items.summary: Nikola Vasic gjorde en känslig flytt när han bytte Hammarby mot Gais. Alin Boudri är istället klar för Bajen. Den allsvenska panelen sågar den moderna fotbollen.

items.title: Värnamo-Ljungskile: 2-3 (0-1)
items.summary: Värnamos omstart under Kim Bergstrand började med en 3–2-förlust hemma mot Ljungskile. Trots tydliga offensiva framsteg och en insats som enligt undertecknad borde ha räckt till minst en poäng, blev de defensiva bristerna återigen avgörande. Med tabelläget som det är börjar tiden rinna ut – och just nu känns en degradering till division 1 mer sannolik än ett nytt kontrakt i Superettan

items.title: Reflektioner efter Hammarby - Degerfors
items.summary: Bajen gör ännu en stark insats och tränare Rydström har nu vunnit sina tre första matcher med bajenmärket på bröstet. En sömnig första halvlek följdes upp med en lekfull och målfarlig andra. Här nedan är reflektioner från matchen

items.title: Inför Sandviken hemma: Ska IFK inleda en ny segersvit
items.summary: Nu är sommaruppehållet äntligen slut och den riktiga fotbollen börjar igen. Huvudrätten under söndagen blir matchen på Parken mot Stålmännen från Sandviken. Bruksorten är platsen där vår tränare Eldars tillbringat större delen av sitt liv, och där han blev formad som tränare.

items.title: Krönika: Mäster Skräddare
items.summary: Denna artikel är byggd kring den klassiska historien ”Mäster Skräddare”, där en man går till skräddaren för att få en rock sydd. Det blir dock ingen rock, utan för varje vecka han kommer dit så blir plagget mindre och mindre, tills det inte blir något alls. Den artikel skall läsas med STOR ironi, då den inte är menad att skada någon på något sätt utan bara få er läsare att tänka till och kanske, eventuellt, få ett gott skratt

items.title: Kafaji klar för London City – Fotbollssurr om övergången
items.summary: Rosa Kafaji är klar för London City Lionesses. Den 23-åriga svenska landslagsspelaren lämnar Arsenal och har skrivit på ett treårskontrakt med den ambitiösa WSL-klubben. Här är vad Linda Fotbollssurr tycker om övergången

items.title: Nyförvärvet Renecke hyllar Bajenfansen: ”Ger 10–20 procent extra”
items.summary: Waylon Renecke, 20, gjorde debut inför Hammarbys supportrar i 4–0-segern mot Degerfors i söndags. Inför morgondagens kvalmatch till Europa League hemma mot belgiska storklubben Anderlecht hyllar han atmosfären på 3 arena. – Det kommer att bli otroligt, säger sydafrikanen till SVT Sport

items.title: Johaugs sorg efter nära vännens död: ”Chock”
items.summary: Idrottsnorge är i sorg efter att en av landets stora idrottsprofiler gått bort. Nu sörjs den dubble OS-mästaren Olaf Tufte av nära vännen Therese Johaug. – Det är svårt att acceptera vad som hänt, säger hon till NRK

items.title: ‘Everyone is hooked on football’: Indian league seeks to ride World Cup wave
items.summary: Club chiefs hope that a revitalised setup can help huge interest in this summer’s tournament translate into growth of the Indian domestic game India was not at the World Cup, but the tournament drew huge interest from within the country . This has happened before but now comes with significant, and necessary, changes in the domestic game that could transform a quadrennial passion into perennial interest. After more than a year of negative headlines regarding the Indian Super League, the country’s top tier will kick off on 4 September.

items.title: How LNZ Cherkasy become a wartime success story with few modern parallels
items.summary: Ukrainian club were in fourth tier in 2021 but, despite war with Russia, will contest European qualifiers for first time It is time for set-piece training at LNZ Cherkasy’s smart, shiny training facility. “Arsenal!” one player says with a laugh as the grappling at a corner begins, the joke spreading among others clustered in the six-yard box. That is a level everyone here must aspire to but they are edging nearer.

items.title: New faces, gulfs in class and flawed drama: how 48 teams changed the World Cup
items.summary: The first 48-team World Cup had its positives and negatives – do we need to brace for a 64-team future? It was less than 12 months after Gianni Infantino succeeded Sepp Blatter as president that Fifa announced the expansion of the men’s World Cup from 32 to 48 teams. Diego Maradona said he was “delighted by Gianni’s initiative” because it allowed each country to dream of reaching the tournament, renewing their passion for football.

items.title: A tournament that crossed borders: crunching the numbers on the diaspora World Cup
items.summary: Nationality in football is increasingly complex and the influence of players from the diaspora has never been higher at a World Cup “Nationality says where you come from. And I actually come from four countries: France, Algeria, Nigeria and Great Britain. I consider myself very lucky to possess these four parts, which all enrich me.” This view was given by Michael Olise.

items.summary: Hammarby jagar europeiskt ligaspel och första hindret är belgiska Anderlecht. Signaturen ”Belgisk Fotboll” på SvenskaFans har stenkoll på Bajens motstånd. – Laget är betydligt svårare att analysera och förutse, säger han

items.title: Panel inför Anderlecht på Nya Söderstadion
items.summary: Då var det äntligen dags för Hammarby att deltaga i Europakval igen, och ikväll torsdag så är det belgiska Anderlecht som står på andra sidan när matchen drar igång 19:00 på Nya Söderstadion. Vi på S@derstadions redaktion har satt ihop en panel med fokus på kvällens match. De som ingår i panelen denna gång är Ludvig Stokki, Per Planhammar och Oliver Jernelius
